item.title: 受沃尔玛支持的PhonePe在地缘政治紧张局势和市场波动之际暂停IPO计划
item.description: 沃尔玛支持的印度金融科技公司PhonePe周一表示，鉴于地缘政治紧张局势以及全球资本市场的波动，该公司已暂时搁置其首次公开募股计划。【原文：Walmart -backed ​Indian ​fintech firm PhonePe ⁠has ​temporarily paused ​its initial public offering ​plans, ​citing geopolitical tensions ‌and ⁠volatility in global capital ​markets, ​the ⁠company said ​on Monday.】

item.title: 裕信银行发起收购德国商业银行股份的要约，旨在重启收购谈判
item.description: 意大利联合信贷银行周一表示，将发起一项非邀约收购，旨在将其在德国商业银行的持股比例提高至30%以上，并迫使这家竞争对手展开合并谈判。【原文：Italy's UniCredit said on Monday it was ​launching an unsolicited offer to increase its stake in ‌Commerzbank to more than 30% and push its rival to engage in merger talks.】

item.title: 英国青少年抵制澳大利亚式的社交媒体禁令
item.description: 英国青少年与国外的同龄人一样，对社交媒体的态度既矛盾又复杂。【原文：British teenagers, like their peers abroad, have a conflicted relationship with social media.】

item.title: 台湾富士康第四季度利润下滑2%，未达预期
item.description: 全球最大的电子代工企业台湾富士康周一公布，其第四季度利润较去年同期下降2.4%，低于市场预期。【原文：Taiwan's Foxconn , the ​world's largest contract ‌electronics maker, reported on Monday a ​2.4% fall ​in fourth-quarter profit versus ⁠the same ​period a year ​earlier, lagging estimates.】

item.title: 日元作为避险货币的光环正在褪色
item.description: 日元是全球首屈一指的避险货币之一——通常在市场动荡时期被预期会走强。那么，面对美国和以色列与伊朗的战争，日元为何表现如此疲软？【原文：The Japanese yen is one of the world's pre-eminent safe haven currencies - typically expected to strengthen in times of market turmoil. So why has it performed so poorly in the face of the U.S. and Israel's war with ​Iran?】

item.title: 随着人工智能热潮导致供应紧张，钌价格创下历史新高
item.description: 分析师和生产商表示，作为铂族金属（PGMs）中的一种次要金属，钌的价格已飙升至历史新高，原因是供应短缺以及与人工智能相关的需求增长导致市场趋紧。【原文：Ruthenium, a minor metal in the platinum-group metals (PGMs), has surged to an all-time ​high as supply constraints and growing demand linked ‌to artificial intelligence tighten the market, analysts and producers said.】

item.title: 中东战事打乱药品空运路线，恐影响抗癌药物供应
item.description: 行业高管表示，中东地区的战事正扰乱了关键药品向海湾地区的供应，危及了抗癌药物及其他需冷藏治疗药物的供应路线，迫使企业改道航班并寻找陆路通道进入该地区。【原文：War in the Middle East is disrupting the flow of critical medicines to the Gulf, imperilling supply routes for cancer drugs and other treatments that require refrigeration and forcing companies to reroute flights ​and find overland access into the region, industry executives said.】

item.title: 晨间竞标：逼近极限总是冒险之举
item.description: 韦恩·科尔为您展望今日欧洲及全球市场走势。【原文：A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Wayne Cole.】

item.title: 网约车应用inDrive表示2025年营收增长31%，计划拓展配送业务
item.description: 网约车应用 inDrive 的创始人兼首席执行官阿尔森·托姆斯基表示，随着每单收益的提升，该公司去年的净收入增长了近三分之一，此前该公司经历了数年的快速扩张。【原文：Ride-hailing application inDrive saw net revenue grow by nearly a third last year, founder and CEO ​Arsen Tomsky said, as profitability per ride improved ‌following several years of rapid expansion.】

item.title: 华纳兄弟在奥斯卡上的胜利喜忧参半，因派拉蒙收购案迫在眉睫
item.description: 华纳兄弟在周日的奥斯卡颁奖典礼上成为最大赢家，尽管其即将以1100亿美元的价格被派拉蒙-Skydance收购一事，给现场气氛蒙上了一层阴影——这笔交易正在重塑好莱坞的制片厂格局。【原文：Warner Bros emerged as the biggest winner at the Academy Awards on Sunday, ​though the mood was clouded by its pending $110 billion sale to Paramount Skydance , a deal reshaping ‌Hollywood's studio landscape.】

item.title: 独家：随着北京加大自给自足力度，中国第二大芯片制造商正准备投产7纳米芯片
item.description: 据四位知情人士透露，中国华鸿集团已研发出可用于生产人工智能芯片的先进芯片制造技术，这标志着北京在提升科技自主能力方面迈出了重要一步。【原文：China's Hua Hong Group has developed advanced chip manufacturing technologies that can be used to produce artificial intelligence chips, four people familiar with the matter said, a major milestone in Beijing's efforts to boost tech self-sufficiency.】

item.title: 美光计划在新收购的台湾厂址建设第二座芯片工厂
item.description: 美国内存芯片制造商美光科技周一表示，计划在台湾通罗园区建设第二家制造工厂，该园区是该公司近期从力晶科技手中收购的。【原文：U.S. memory chipmaker Micron Technology ​said on Monday it ‌plans to build a second manufacturing facility in ​Taiwan at the Tongluo site ​it recently acquired from Powerchip Semiconductor ⁠Manufacturing Corp .】

item.title: 中美双方正努力在巴黎就贸易管理及农业协议达成共识，为习近平与特朗普的峰会做准备
item.description: 据熟悉谈判情况的消息人士透露，中美两国高级经济官员原定于周一在巴黎结束会谈，双方在农业、关键矿产和贸易管理等领域可能达成共识，这些议题或将在北京由美国总统唐纳德·特朗普和中国国家主席习近平进一步讨论。【原文：Top U.S. and Chinese economic officials were due to ​conclude talks in Paris on Monday, with potential areas of agreement in agriculture, critical minerals and managed trade that could be taken up by U.S. President Donald ‌Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing, sources familiar with the discussions said.】

item.title: 随着战争持续，亚洲股市表现谨慎，油价波动剧烈
item.description: 周一，亚洲市场情绪谨慎，因海湾地区的紧张局势导致油价居高不下，这使得通胀前景更加复杂，预计这将促使大多数央行在本周的政策会议上按兵不动，但可能会有一次加息。【原文：Asian markets were in a wary mood on Monday as hostilities in the Gulf kept oil prices elevated, complicating an inflation outlook that ​should keep most central banks on pause at policy meetings this week, barring one possible hike.】

item.title: 京东在欧洲推出Joybuy，直指亚马逊
item.description: 中国电商巨头京东（JD.com）周一在英国、德国、法国、荷兰、比利时和卢森堡推出了其“Joybuy”在线商城，以此加速拓展海外市场，并瞄准市场领导者亚马逊。【原文：Chinese e‑commerce giant JD.com on Monday launched its ​Joybuy online marketplace in the UK, Germany, France, the Netherlands, Belgium ‌and Luxembourg, stepping up its push beyond its home market and taking aim at market leader Amazon .】

item.title: 受中东冲突威胁出口设施影响，油价延续涨势
item.description: 随着美以联军对伊朗的战争进入第三周，油价周一延续涨势。这场战争使石油基础设施面临风险，并导致霍尔木兹海峡持续封锁，造成全球石油供应遭遇有史以来最严重的中断。【原文：Oil prices extended gains on Monday as the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran entered a third week, ‌putting oil infrastructure at risk and keeping the Strait of Hormuz shut in the biggest disruption to global supplies ever.】

item.title: 澳大利亚的Syrah公司与特斯拉将解决石墨供应协议涉嫌违约问题的期限延长
item.description: 澳大利亚石墨矿商Syrah Resources周一表示，已与特斯拉达成协议，将解决双方石墨供应协议中涉嫌违约问题的最后期限第四次延长至6月1日。【原文：Australian graphite miner Syrah Resources said on Monday it agreed with Tesla to extend for a fourth time a ​deadline to resolve an alleged default under their ‌graphite supply agreement to June 1.】

item.title: 澳大利亚莱纳斯公司与美国签署稀土氧化物供应协议
item.description: 澳大利亚莱纳斯稀土公司周一表示，其子公司莱纳斯美国有限责任公司已与美国政府签署了一份具有约束力的意向书，以敲定一项稀土氧化物供应协议。【原文：Australia's Lynas Rare Earths said on Monday its unit Lynas USA ​LLC has signed a binding letter of intent ‌with the U.S. government to finalise a rare earth oxide supply agreement.】

item.title: 独家：消息人士称，中美在“异常平稳”的巴黎会谈中讨论了农产品及贸易管理问题
item.description: 据两位知情人士透露，周日，中美两国高级经济官员在巴黎举行了“异常平稳”的会谈，讨论了农业、关键矿产以及贸易管理等可能达成共识的领域，供美国总统唐纳德·特朗普和中国国家主席习近平在北京会晤时参考。【原文：Top ​U.S. and Chinese economic officials held "remarkably stable" talks in Paris on Sunday that touched on potential areas ‌of agreement in agriculture, critical minerals and managed trade for U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping to consider in Beijing, two sources familiar with the talks said.】

item.title: Perpetual将以3.5亿美元的预付款将财富管理业务出售给贝恩资本
item.description: 澳大利亚永续集团（Perpetual Limited）周一表示，已同意将其财富管理业务出售给私募股权公司贝恩资本（Bain Capital），交易对价为5亿澳元（合3.5亿美元）的预付现金。【原文：Australia's Perpetual Limited said ​on Monday it agreed to sell ‌its wealth management business to private equity firm Bain Capital for an upfront cash payment of ​A$500 million ($350 million).】

item.title: 恒天然首席执行官迈尔斯·赫雷尔将卸任
item.description: 新西兰乳业公司恒天然集团（Fonterra Co-operative Group）周一表示，首席执行官迈尔斯·赫雷尔（Miles Hurrell）已辞职，此前他担任该职位八年，并在公司工作了25年。【原文：New Zealand dairy company Fonterra Co-operative ‌Group said on Monday that Chief Executive Officer Miles Hurrell had resigned after ​eight years in the role and ​a 25-year career with the company.】

item.title: 伯格姆表示，亚太盟友与美国企业签署了价值570亿美元的协议
item.description: 美国内政部长道格·伯格姆周日表示，在本周末于东京举行的印太能源安全论坛期间，亚太地区盟国与美国企业达成了22项总价值570亿美元的协议。【原文：Asia-Pacific allies agreed to $57 ​billion in 22 deals with American companies ‌during this weekend's Indo-Pacific Energy Security Forum in Tokyo, U.S. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said on Sunday.】

item.title: 蒂尔在罗马举行的秘密会议引起了教会的关注
item.description: 美国亿万富翁、风险投资家、唐纳德·特朗普总统的早期支持者彼得·蒂尔于周日在罗马启动了一系列闭门讲座，探讨“敌基督”的概念，此举引发了天主教评论家的关注。【原文：Peter Thiel, the U.S. billionaire venture capitalist and early supporter of President Donald ​Trump, launched on Sunday a series of closed-door lectures in Rome exploring the concept of the ‌Antichrist, drawing scrutiny from Catholic commentators.】

item.title: 美国航空公司首席执行官敦促国会结束僵局，向机场安检人员支付薪资
item.description: 美国各大航空公司的首席执行官周日敦促国会迅速采取行动，结束这场已持续29天的政府部分停摆，此次停摆迫使5万名机场安检人员无薪工作，并警告称这可能会进一步扰乱美国的航空旅行。【原文：The CEOs of major U.S. airlines urged Congress on Sunday to move quickly to end a 29-day partial government shutdown that has forced 50,000 airport ​security officers to work without pay, warning it could further disrupt U.S. air ‌travel.】

item.title: 中东冲突威胁出口设施，油价有望进一步上涨
item.description: 随着美以联军对伊朗的战争进入第三周，石油基础设施面临风险，霍尔木兹海峡持续封锁，导致全球最大规模的供应中断，油价周一开盘或将延续涨势。【原文：Oil prices could extend gains at Monday's open as the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran entered a third week, putting oil infrastructure at risk and keeping the Strait of Hormuz shut in the world's largest supply disruption.】

item.title: 日本将释放石油储备，美国则呼吁购买美国产品
item.description: 日本计划于周一开始动用战略石油储备，以缓解美以联军对伊朗开战带来的冲击，这令人不禁想起半个世纪前那场促使东京建立战略储备的石油危机。【原文：Japan plans ​to start releasing oil from its stockpiles on Monday to soften the shock from the ‌U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, a stark reminder of the oil crisis half a century ago that prompted Tokyo to create reserves.】

item.title: 据CNBC报道，费蒂塔娱乐公司正就以65亿美元收购凯撒娱乐进行谈判
item.description: 据CNBC周六援引知情人士消息报道，蒂尔曼·费尔蒂塔旗下的费尔蒂塔娱乐公司正就以每股32美元的价格收购凯撒娱乐进行谈判，该交易对凯撒娱乐的估值为65亿美元。【原文：Tilman Fertitta's ​Fertitta Entertainment ‌is negotiating ​to ​buy Caesars Entertainment ⁠for $32 ​per share, ​at an equity value ​of $6.5 ​billion, CNBC reported ‌on ⁠Saturday, citing sources ​close ​to ⁠the situation.】

item.title: 据《The Information》报道，字节跳动因版权纠纷暂停推出视频AI模型
item.description: 据《The Information》周六援引两名直接知情人士的消息报道，在与好莱坞各大电影公司及流媒体平台发生一系列版权纠纷后，字节跳动已暂停其最新视频生成模型Seedance 2.0的全球发布。【原文：ByteDance has put ​on hold ‌the global launch of ​its latest ​video-generation model, Seedance ⁠2.0, ​after a series ​of copyright disputes with major ​Hollywood ​studios and streaming platforms, ‌The ⁠Information reported on Saturday, citing ​two ​people ⁠with direct knowledge ​of the ​situation.】

item.title: 马斯克表示，特斯拉的“巨型”芯片工厂项目将于七天后启动
item.description: 特斯拉首席执行官埃隆·马斯克周六表示，Terafab项目将于七天后启动。【原文：Tesla CEO ​Elon Musk ‌said on Saturday ​that the ​Terafab project will ⁠launch ​in seven ​days.】

item.title: 日本和韩国部长表示，两国已做好应对外汇市场波动的准备
item.description: 日本和韩国周六对本国货币的急速贬值表示担忧，并表示已准备好采取行动应对外汇市场的过度波动。【原文：Japan and South Korea expressed concern on Saturday about the rapid declines in their currencies, saying they ​were ready to act against excessive foreign-exchange volatility.】
